topic: Internal Reporting and Risk Data Aggregation — Expectation E6
content: Expectation 6 of the ECB Guide states that institutions are expected to report aggregated risk data that reflect their exposure to climate-related and environmental risks, with a view to enabling the management body and relevant sub-committees to make informed decisions. This requires building data infrastructure capable of aggregating climate risk exposures across the portfolio, including sectoral concentration analysis (e.g. exposure to fossil fuels, carbon-intensive manufacturing, agriculture), geographic risk mapping for physical risk (e.g. flood zones, wildfire-prone areas), counterparty-level climate risk scores or ratings, and portfolio-level emission metrics. The ECB expects internal reports to be produced on a regular basis (at minimum quarterly for key indicators) and to be presented at management body or risk committee level. Reports should include both backward-looking metrics (current exposures, realised losses) and forward-looking indicators (scenario analysis results, trend projections). Data quality and completeness are critical: the ECB has noted that many institutions struggle with data gaps, particularly for Scope 3 emissions of counterparties and physical risk geolocation of collateral.
